agents.name: Meridian
agents.purpose: The Chief. Post-engagement evaluator. Scores right agent used (1-5) + output quality (1-5) after every session. If score < 4, appends refinement note to agent vault file. The floor's constant presence.

agents.name: Chief of Staff
agents.purpose: Cross-project ops lead. Owns the master backlog AND the agent roster. Syncs project backlogs into Backlog.md, keeps dashboard-data.json current, and flags when agent-floor.html needs a DEFS update after new agents are added to the vault.

agents.name: 513Sips Board
agents.purpose: Four-persona strategic review board — CEO (Marcus), CFO (Danny), Hard Sell (Rachel), Brand Director (Vivian). Run before major decisions. Pulls in SME agents for deep-dives.

agents.name: Finance & Tax
agents.purpose: Tax strategy, complex accounting, multi-year financial planning. Consult for Board reviews and year-end prep.

agents.name: Bookkeeper
agents.purpose: Day-to-day accounting: log expenses, reconcile vendor payments, calculate owner reimbursement, check cash flow. Keeps Books clean between finance sessions.

agents.name: Pricing
agents.purpose: Quote generation, package building, margin analysis. Cross-reference Pricing-Strategy note for current rates.

agents.name: Contract
agents.purpose: Contract generation and management. Legal language for customer and vendor agreements.

agents.name: Operations Manager
agents.purpose: Day-to-day ops, scheduling, vendor coordination. Event logistics and supply chain.

agents.name: Marketing
agents.purpose: Brand voice and campaigns. Messaging strategy, positioning, campaign briefs.

agents.name: Quote
agents.purpose: Client quote generation. Builds packages from event specs using current pricing model.

agents.name: Event Recap
agents.purpose: Post-event documentation. Captures event details, notes, and outcomes for the record.

agents.name: Drink / Mixology
agents.purpose: Mixology brain: drink recommendations, quantities, batch prep, event drink logs.

agents.name: Event Post-Mortem
agents.purpose: 28-question guided debrief after every event. Captures what worked, what didn't, and what to improve.

agents.name: Customer Acquisition
agents.purpose: Lead channels, venue scouting, Google reviews management, outreach templates. Drives new business pipeline.

agents.name: Social Media
agents.purpose: Instagram content from Cloudinary photos + blog posts. Competitive analysis against PoppinBar and CincySips.

agents.name: Creative Director
agents.purpose: Design team lead. Translates Marketing briefs into visual direction. Orchestrates Art Director + Copywriter, reviews their work, and is the final gate before anything reaches Eddie.

agents.name: Art Director
agents.purpose: Visual composition, photo selection, layout direction, and hierarchy for carousels and reels. Works from Creative Director's brief.

agents.name: Copywriter
agents.purpose: Headlines, body copy, captions, hashtags — all written content on design deliverables. Brand voice custodian.

agents.name: Design QA
agents.purpose: 3-lens review on every deliverable: Functional (does it work?), UX (is it clear?), Customer Mindset (would they act?). Nothing reaches Eddie until QA signs off.

agents.name: Travel Planner PM
agents.purpose: Feature roadmap, sprint prioritization, and product decisions for the Travel Planner app.

agents.name: Travel Planner Dev
agents.purpose: Technical architecture and implementation for the Travel Planner app. React + TypeScript + Vercel stack.

agents.name: River Hayes
agents.purpose: Overlanding advisor and route intelligence for Charlie's Western Adventure. Dog logistics, dispersed camping, timing windows, crowd strategy. The trip's local guide persona.

agents.name: Engagement Manager
agents.purpose: Orchestrates the full pre-call workflow. Give it a prospect name + URL and it sequences brand research, site audit, and competitor analysis into a ready-to-use call package.

agents.name: Brand Intelligence
agents.purpose: Reviews a prospect's website, Facebook, and Instagram. Outputs tone, culture, platform, customer profile, and recent business activity.

agents.name: Site Audit
agents.purpose: Audits a prospect's site for broken links, SEO issues, missing CTAs, mobile problems, page speed, and outdated content. Outputs a scored report.

agents.name: Competitor Analysis
agents.purpose: Identifies 2–3 direct competitors, benchmarks their sites against the prospect's, and produces a gap summary showing where the prospect is losing ground.

agents.name: Callout Generator
agents.purpose: Produces the 'we did our homework' specific observations for first calls — the hook that opens doors. Transforms audit findings into memorable, personalized callouts.

agents.name: Pricing
agents.purpose: Scopes the project into Refresh / Rebuild / Transform tier + investment estimate from site audit findings. Outputs the investment range Eddie presents on the call.

agents.name: Proposal Writer
agents.purpose: Assembles the full pre-call package — brand intel, audit, competitor gaps, callouts, and investment — into one clean document Eddie hands over or screens.

agents.name: Prototype
agents.purpose: Builds lo-fi HTML prototypes tailored to high-value prospects to make the vision tangible before the call. Increases close rate on Rebuild+ engagements.

agents.name: Design Inspiration
agents.purpose: Surfaces current design trends by industry from showcase sites before any prototyping begins. Grounds the prototype in what's working right now.

agents.name: Standards
agents.purpose: Source of truth for deliverable templates, tone rules, scoring rubrics, pricing tiers, and prototype layout standards. Every other WebGrowth agent references this.

agents.name: Brain Librarian
agents.purpose: Save protocol enforcement, deduplication, vault maintenance. Keeps the Obsidian Brain clean and current.

agents.name: Session Summarizer
agents.purpose: Session handoff generation. Creates the closing summary saved to Brain at the end of every session.

agents.name: Social Media Manager
agents.purpose: Cross-project social media principles. Project-specific agents (513Sips) handle execution.

meridianLog.sessionTheme: Design team engagement — 513Sips carousel (dry-hire content pipeline)
meridianLog.refinementNote: Brand score deferred — final carousel output not yet reviewed by Meridian; re-evaluate on delivery.

tips.current: Your master backlog drifts from project backlogs between sessions — completed work stays open, new items go untracked.
tips.better: Open any session with: 'Act as Chief of Staff, run a backlog sync.' Takes 2 minutes and keeps the dashboard honest. Make it a habit before picking your next task.

tips.current: Blog posts 2, 3, and 4 are past their scheduled publish dates. Three pieces of finished content sitting unpublished.
tips.better: Spend 15 minutes: open 513sips-tools, uncomment the three blog cards in blog/index.html, push to main. Content is already written — this is pure execution.

tips.current: Leads go cold silently. No record of last contact, no way to know when to follow up.
tips.better: Communication Log is the highest-CEO-priority Books feature. Even a simple note field per lead gives you 80% of the value. Start there before building the full schema.

tips.current: Meridian is now built into the agent floor — but the post-engagement scoring habit isn't established yet.
tips.better: After this session, pause and run Meridian scoring. Right agent (1-5), quality (1-5). If anything scores below 4, note the refinement. It takes 90 seconds and compounds over time.

tips.current: All 10 agents are built and validated. The pipeline has never run on a real prospect.
tips.better: The first real engagement will reveal gaps no amount of planning uncovers. Pick any business you've noticed has a weak site. Run it through Engagement Manager. Outcome matters less than pipeline validation.

tips.current: Past clients who gave verbal testimonials haven't been redirected to Google Reviews.
tips.better: Get the Place ID (5 minutes in Maps), generate the direct review link, then text 3-5 past clients with a short personal ask. Google reviews compound — one ask now is worth 10 later.

tips.current: Sessions start with Claude asking clarifying questions before getting to work.
tips.better: Open with: 'Working on [X]. Last session: [Y]. Today: [Z]. Start with [first task].' Eliminates warm-up entirely — first pass is production-quality.

bestPractices.title: Chief of Staff as Session Opener
bestPractices.body: Before any work session, spend 2 minutes with Chief of Staff. Read the sync report. Know what's actually P1. Then open your working session with full context — no surprises mid-session.

bestPractices.title: Meridian Scoring Habit
bestPractices.body: At session end: Right agent (1-5)? Output quality (1-5)? If either < 4, one sentence on what to improve. Saves to vault. Feeds agent refinement over time. 90 seconds of investment per session.

bestPractices.title: Opus 4 vs Sonnet vs Haiku
bestPractices.body: Opus: Board reviews, pricing strategy, legal review, complex architecture. Sonnet: builds, saves, blog work, domain agents, design pipeline. Haiku: quick lookups, formatting, data transformation, routine logging.

bestPractices.title: Agent Prompt Structure
bestPractices.body: Best agents have: (1) Role + persona, (2) Scope boundaries — what they do AND don't, (3) Output format, (4) 2–3 example invocations. Skip any and outputs get inconsistent.

bestPractices.title: Session Handoff Value
bestPractices.body: Write handoffs as if a stranger is picking up tomorrow with no context. What do they absolutely need to know? Everything else is noise. The handoff is the session's entire lasting value.
